calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar May-27-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: ATTY. CHARLES O. MORGAN, JR., TEL. 292-8030, FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. SHANNON S. LINDSAY OF REUBEN & JUNIUS LLP, TEL. 567-9000, FOR RESPONDENT TERRY BROWN;
ATTY. WILLIAM SWANK OF HOLME, ROBERTS & OWEN LLP, TEL.(213)-572-4300, APPEARING THROUGH COURT CALL FOR RESPONDENT MICHAEL KILROY.

RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER ROBERT MANN'S Petition To Compel Arbitration. BROWN AND 600 ALABAMA ARE ORDERED TO ARBITRATE BECAUSE BROWN SIGNED THE AGREEMENT THAT INCLUDED THE ARBITRATION PROVISION, DID SO AS A MEMBER OF 600 ALABAMA, AND ENJOYS THE BENEFITS OF THE AGREEMENT. KILROY WHO IS NEITHER A PARTY TO THE AGREEMENT, WHICH IS EXPRESSLY ONLY BETWEEN THE OWNERS, NOR A SIGNATORY OF ANYTHING OTHER THAN A PERSONAL GUARANTEE THAT APPEARS SEPARATELY AND NEITHER INCORPORATES THE AGREEMENT NOR ITSELF CONTAINS AN ARBITRATION PROVISION IS NOT REQUIRED TO ARBITRATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 =(301/PJB/LB)
